# Writing wpm docs

Every command in `cli/command/` has a matching reference file in
`docs/cli/`.

Nested commands use underscores:

- `wpm auth` → `docs/cli/auth.md`
- `wpm auth login` → `docs/cli/auth_login.md`

## File structure

Each reference file has two regions:

```markdown
# wpm install

<!---MARKER_GEN_START-->

(auto-generated: Short, Aliases, Options, Subcommands table)

<!---MARKER_GEN_END-->

## Description

(hand-written prose)

## Examples

(hand-written shell sessions)
```

### Auto-generated region

Everything between:

```html
<!---MARKER_GEN_START-->
<!---MARKER_GEN_END-->
```

is generated by `./scripts/docs/generate-md`.

Do not edit this section by hand.

To change:

- aliases
- option defaults
- command metadata
- flag descriptions

edit the Go source under `cli/command/<name>/` and regenerate the docs.

### Hand-written region

Everything outside the markers is preserved across regenerations.

Write all explanatory content here, including:

- caveats
- examples
- workflows
- troubleshooting notes
- conceptual explanations

## Supported sections

The docs generator reads exactly two sections from each file:

| Section          | Output            |
| ---------------- | ----------------- |
| `## Description` | Main body content |
| `## Examples`    | Examples section  |

Additional top-level sections are ignored by the docs site and manpage
generator.

For example:

- `## Notes`
- `## Caveats`
- `## See also`

still appear on GitHub, but are not included in generated output.

Use nested headings inside `## Description` when needed:

```markdown
## Description

### Authentication flow

#### Token storage
```

Avoid introducing new top-level `##` sections.

## Workflow

1. Add or modify the `cobra.Command` in `cli/command/<name>/`.

2. Run:

   ```sh
   ./scripts/docs/generate-md
   ```

3. Edit:
   - `## Description`
   - `## Examples`

   in `docs/cli/<name>.md`.

4. Commit the Go changes and Markdown updates together.

## Verifying generated output

After editing docs, regenerate all derived artifacts:

```sh
./scripts/docs/generate-md
./scripts/docs/generate-yaml
```

Then inspect the generated YAML:

```text
docs/yaml/wpm_<command>.yaml
```

Verify that:

- `long:` contains the `## Description` content
- `examples:` contains the `## Examples` content

If either field is empty:

- the heading may be misspelled
- the heading level may be incorrect
- the section may be inside the generated marker block

Fix the Markdown and regenerate.

## Other reference docs

Non-CLI topics live in their own directories under `docs/`.

Each topic directory contains an `index.md` overview plus any related pages:

```text
docs/
├── README.md
├── cli/
├── wpm-json/
│   ├── index.md
│   ├── dependencies.md
│   └── scripts.md
└── wpmignore/
    ├── index.md
    └── patterns.md
```

These files are plain Markdown.

Unlike CLI reference docs:

- there are no generated marker blocks
- there is no regeneration workflow
- content is edited entirely by hand

Use these directories for conceptual documentation, specifications, guides,
and other non-command reference material.
